WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL

Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Gem Inlaid Gauntlets
Lore Item
Slot: HANDS
AC: 8
DEX: +3 STA: +3
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Lore:Gloves Crafted by Battlemore
Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Chain
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):0, 0, 0
Stackable:No

Page Updated:Sat Oct 25th, 2025

Expansion: Original
Average Price: 250pp Pricing Data...
Rarity: Rare
Level to Attain: 30
[Comments ]

This item is the result of a quest.
Expansion List - Premium only.
Quest Name
Gem Inlaid Gauntlets

Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
South Qeynos

stats

Post Comment
Not good since Luclin
QuoteReply
# Oct 20 2025 at 3:54 AM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
I guess if you're on a TLP server, before Luclin drops, these are OK, but once the newbie armors enter the game, you can get better with less difficulty.
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?
▲ Page top
A nice upgrade to Split Paw Gloves on TLP
QuoteReply
# Aug 24 2020 at 5:35 PM Rating: Excellent
stickleback
Guru
153 posts
Ran this quest with my lowbie monk on Aradune to get something magic to wear, ended up buying a pair of Spit Paw Gloves while waiting for the final scroll (all bought for a few silver from merchants in S Qeynos so worth checking their stock if you aren't ready to fight the gnolls in SK) but these are better, and half the weight.

As with other posts, had trouble finding Durkis who was hiding the other side of a mountain top on the south side of the road to Kaladin (used /tar Durkis as a social bound to my A / D keys to find him eventually. Thanks to the players that dueled me in the Qeynos arena to (free) port me back to my bindpoint in Freeport

Updated item pics
▲ Page top
Location of Durkis Battlemore
QuoteReply
# Mar 29 2005 at 12:18 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591834311960
Scholar
53 posts
I have completed this quest 3 times. Only one of those times has Durkis been in his correct home location - the first guard house as you travel the road going southwest from Kaladim. The other 2 times I had to track him and found him stuck up on a mountain. I think he must get stuck here trying to get back to his house and cannot get over the mountain as there is a zone edge there.

At any rate, I found him at loc 1600, 1100. If you don't find him in the guard house look in this area.

Another note: these gauntlets sell for around 150 pp on Torvonilous as of this posting.
▲ Page top
Missing Man?!
QuoteReply
# Sep 20 2003 at 5:30 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1694391393584
19 posts
Whered the Turn in Erudite in Crows Tavern Go??

I got the 4 Skins and No Turn in Fella...

After waiting an Hour in hopes of a "spawning" i Destroyed the first 3 and stuck #4 in the bank(i bought all but 4 cause 1-3 always Dropped where as the 4th was rare)

Anyways back to my question, WHERE THE FRICK IS THAT ERUDITE?!?!

They moved him. If I remember correctly, he's in the Herb Jar now in South Qeynos (#10 on the eqatlas map) or the wizard guild next door (#11). But I think it's #10.

QuoteReply
# Feb 19 2003 at 9:14 PM Rating: Decent

Got all 4 scrolls now. Bought 2-4 from the centaur merchant for less than a plat each. Then a kind Barbarian Shaman just gave me the first scroll when I asked if he had one for sale.
Gotta love Saryrn server!

Warning
QuoteReply
# Jan 11 2003 at 1:44 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1652660341212
19 posts
I did this quest last night. First it should be known that I am a Vah Shir Beastlord and I had a Gnome Necro friend alot doing the quest. Everything went smoothly until I tried to turn the note into the Dwarf. I was too big to get in the doorway. Fortunatly a good druid turned me into a wolf and I was able to complete the quest. My Necro friend however was unable to turn in the note due to KOS faction with the Dwarves. Invis and wolf form both were inefective.

Also I did this at 25 and maybe could have been done lower if you have a good group

Hope this helps

Sphinxy
25 Beastlord
Fennin Ro

Edited, Sat Jan 11 13:21:52 2003

#REDACTED, Posted: Nov 26 2002 at 12:51 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Yep these suck.
Scrolling for Scrolls
QuoteReply
# Mar 12 2002 at 12:59 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253669899
3 posts
I must be one of the most hated people in SK right now.. for the sheer fact that my 8th level Shaman Barbarian has collected all the numbered Scrolls (and a backpack of Page-Scrolls!) as well as a good few FS Weapons, Gnoll Hide Gloves.. Split Paws as well as SP Eyes to sell back in Newb Area Blackburrow.. and to boot, I can manage to get myself some gauntlets without any trouble.
Ayonae Ro server has the kindest people ever.. especially with Mr. Ninja Loot wandering around asking if he can have the corpse!

Aetherine
Barbarian Shaman of the 8th Circle
Ayonae Ro!
▲ Page top
RE: Scrolling for Scrolls
QuoteReply
# Jun 25 2002 at 10:43 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591776948404
11 posts
Ayonae Ro is a good server with some kind people on it. but even the kindest hearts get annoyed with beggers ;-) so just be careful.

i.e., i've had my lvl 28 (almost 29, one more till 30!) ranger in SK since ooh.....25? I think Paludal on luclin is best exp up till 25, killing in the camps. but it's starting to become a but crowded, and ppl some to have trouble sharing the bandit camps. i hit 25 and come down, started in SK. Already had these gauntlets, thinking about picking up another pair to sell or give to me alts...any, back to my story lol. i was in SK just last night, running around with a few guildies (2 other rng's). we took down that named centuar that drops the runed oak bow. kianie (one of my guildies) looted it, and she gave me her old trueshot. i gave my darkwood recurve to some 18 rng. he was very ungrateful, didn't even say thanks, we even buffed him some. never give him anything again. :-)
▲ Page top
RE: Scrolling for Scrolls
QuoteReply
# Mar 22 2002 at 12:57 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591772546383
11 posts
lol, grats man. i lvled at paw from 24 to 36 and the amount of loot that wrots there is amazing, especially since only five to ten lvls lower the it takes almost ten times as many kills to get as worthy loot (such as FS). i was pissed at myself for not going there as a newbie and doing exactly what it sounds like you did, just picking up the wrotting loot ). good idea man, you will go far =)
▲ Page top
Greetings
QuoteReply
# Nov 16 2001 at 8:30 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591755919759
16 posts
I did this quest to upgrade from my raw silk gloves. Alas, I did it at lvl 49, so I'm sure there are much better gloves out there, especially for a caster. When I have the time, I will get better gloves. I had problems finding the 3rd scroll. I was able to finally purchase it for 50pp off of a kind ranger.
▲ Page top
Great Quest
QuoteReply
# Sep 25 2001 at 9:46 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361706327
13 posts
I got these at level 20! I asked a group of 25 to 30's who were camping outside if I could loot the scrolls. I lent a hand as I could, and eventually they invited me into the group.

Two hours later I had all four scrolls, 20 or 30 plat in loot, 2 yellows of exp, and then a big long runabout for the gaunts! :)

I highly recommend this quest, the gaunts are awesome!
▲ Page top
RE: Great Quest
QuoteReply
# Jan 02 2002 at 6:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771853151
153 posts
Did the same thing, Aedon. Camping the spires near Paw in your 20's is great for exp and loot. Collecting the scrolls is just something you can do without really thinking about it. Once you get all four, travelling to and from Qeynos, Freeport and, eventually, Butcherblock is well worth the time.

Easy quest to complete and nice reward.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 02 2002 at 6:55 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Edited, Wed Jan 2 18:57:15 2002
#Anonymous, Posted: Aug 31 2001 at 10:21 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) 250-300? ripping people off, its barly worth 150

# Sep 25 2001 at 9:42 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1593361706327
13 posts

▲ Page top
Druids
QuoteReply
# Aug 21 2001 at 12:34 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766145533
8 posts
This is a very easy quest, and i recommend it to druids because you can bind yourself by the guy in qeynos you give scrolls to, tp yourself to wc, run to east freeport, hand the book in there, gate to guy in qeynos, hand in book, and then tp yourself to bbm and run to durkis battlemore. It can be done in 10min, and can be sold for about 250-300pp.

Laeledar
Ranger of the 22 circle
Tribunal
▲ Page top
RE: Druids
QuoteReply
# Sep 15 2001 at 4:14 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966040433
29 posts
this is true as far as it goes but getting the 4 scrolls can take days - I know I did it twice (once to wear and once to sell) and the second time someone gave me two of the scrolls while I looted the other two. These items are also needed for another quest (forgot which) so they are popular.
▲ Page top
RE: Druids
QuoteReply
# Dec 05 2001 at 10:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762323414
109 posts
I take all my Low to Mid-20's Characters to SK to hunt. The people who say these scrolls take days to get must be really unlucky. I have taken 3 chars through SK doing the gnolls in my 20's and these scrolls drop like MAD! I usually have all four scrolls on each of my chars within 1 or 2 hours of gnoll bashing. The only reason that it may take so long to get is b/c everyone loots the dern things before you get the chance =\

Just my 2cp

Bristlebane Server
45 SK
28 Enc
24 Ran
17 Wiz
bla
bla
▲ Page top
RE: Druids
QuoteReply
# Nov 26 2001 at 10:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592251440449
1 post
I admit I completely got lucky, but I was able to get all four scrolls within an hour. I had a group of six and we were able to continualy fight and every so often one popped up on a corpse. And as a druid, the run was even easier.

Dilph
Druid of the 24th

# Nov 09 2001 at 2:34 AM Rating: Decent
Jophiel
View User Forum
Liberal Conspiracy
TILT
The scrolls are also used in the Tear of Karana quest, a +STA earring you get in Qeynos. Used to be where you needed all four scrolls for the earring a la the gauntlets, but now you only need one scroll for the earring (any of the four will do).

Belkira wrote:
Wow. Regular ol' Joph fan club in here.
▲ Page top
WARNING!
QuoteReply
# Jun 23 2001 at 12:39 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592245744252
63 posts
The alst part of the quest is delivering the note to Durkis in BB. He is in the back of a small building near Kaladim along the trail to the docks. If you are playing a large character (Barbarian, Ogre, or Troll) you will not be able to fit in the door, and since the note is No Drop you will have wasted your time. I learned this one the hard way. The note is the only item that is No Drop.
▲ Page top
RE: WARNING!
QuoteReply
# Dec 24 2001 at 10:57 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592252761301
13 posts
Did you ever Try Ducking?

ive sen barbarians get in to gnome huts that way

if your wondering its the d key on the KEYBOARD


Zhelot
12th season Ranger
Fironia Vie Srever

▲ Page top
RE: WARNING!
QuoteReply
# Sep 26 2001 at 9:52 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
If you are a rogue and have the mask of deception change to dark elf form and enter the door (be sure to be hidden). Once inside you can either wait for illusion to wear off or log off/on. You are than close enough to Durkis for the trade. Cast the illusion again in the corner and sneak/hide your way out of gaurds view.

15 posts
Shrink spell can't be cast outdoors. Buy a Shrink potion, that will do the trick.
▲ Page top
RE: WARNING!
QuoteReply
# Apr 08 2003 at 9:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591787840479
4 posts
Thanks to a patch, shamans can now cast shrink outdoors. So just get a shaman friend to cast it on you. Being a Vah shir shaman, i know that this spell comes in real handy in all they little peoples villages.
▲ Page top
RE: WARNING!
QuoteReply
# Jul 14 2001 at 3:32 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Shrink should work, since you can't cast SoW in Kaladim...that makes it an inside zone.

Worst case, use a potion which should also work.

And lastly, try the F9 views...you might get one that will allow you to trade with the NPC.
▲ Page top
RE: WARNING!
QuoteReply
# Jul 30 2001 at 8:33 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591758061644
185 posts
it is in BB, near Kaladim, not in kaladim. But wolf form or a shrink potion will do the job just fine.

# Jun 25 2001 at 4:12 PM Rating: Excellent

QuoteReply
# May 31 2001 at 12:02 PM Rating: Default

View User Forum
Anonymous
Im a chanter and Im wondering where to bind myself to ge tthe fastest time to completion? I already have a friend that can tp me to WC if i bind myself in qeynos, so that seems the most logical place to bind, any suggestions?
▲ Page top
Soloing at 20... yeah right...
QuoteReply
# May 29 2001 at 8:48 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
No way you were soloing these gnolls at 20 without having so much dwontime it took forever to complete... In a group... no problem at 20, but soloing would be very difficult for any class... All lvl 20 speels are going to be resisited more times than they hit on these guys... At lvl 23 My bard has most things resisted except for Largo's binding... Even bruscos is resisted 7 times out of 10... the the master kiter of a bard would take forever to kill these without a group... Druids spells and Wizzie spells would resist... A necro may be able to pet one to death if they ahve enough bone chips... A lvl 20 Warrior would get rocked trying to solo it... (in my group a fully twinked 24 Warrior with full set of crafted and An Ex Axe was still losing half his HP with 4 ppl in our group before we could kill a gnoll and that was with largos binding and Anthem on)
▲ Page top
RE: Soloing at 20... yeah right...
QuoteReply
# Aug 24 2001 at 10:41 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764799758
5 posts
Yeah 20 might be a little low, but I know 23 isn't (if you cast) I wouldn't be able to do it at 23 do to my low damage nuke, and my low hit points :), however, when I fought them in a group I never had any resists, except the occasional resist on snare (but I only got them as often as when I'm fighting blues, so that really doesn't say anything) So if you can keep them at a distance, and do not run out of mana, you should be fine.

Durnik
23 Ranger
Elzidar
12 Wizard

Zebuxoruk
▲ Page top
RE: Soloing at 20... yeah right...
QuoteReply
# Aug 21 2001 at 12:05 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591756932547
36 posts
ok, if that warrior with crafted was lost half of his HP if he was that "twinked" is wrong, and you might want to try, if your trying to solo paw or at the spires, a fungi tunic. if that is too hard for you to get save up about 2k - 3k for a iksar ceramony chest plate AKA iksar regen CP, that should help with the down time if you are a warrior
▲ Page top
RE: Soloing at 20... yeah right...
QuoteReply
# Aug 03 2001 at 5:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361412976
12 posts
My enchanters spells at levl 20 never resisted
▲ Page top
RE: Soloing at 20... yeah right...
QuoteReply
# Jul 11 2001 at 12:50 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591762569836
4 posts
Ok, just because people say that Bard's are the master kiter's dosen't mean that they actually or, or that it makes them a good solo class. As a Druid at level 21 I was soloing in Splitpaw and getting the scrolls. Before I decided to attack there I got my fist set of GIG's at level 19 when I got my ports and Superior Camo. Grasping Roots, Snare, Stinging Swarm and DD until the Root breaks and re-root. Occcasionaly Snare was resisted and sometimes my DD didn't hit for full but I had no problem at all hunting there. Want to know why a Bard is NOT the Master Kiter? You DoT is AoE, your Root/Snare is non-exisitent, and your DD is nowhere near as powerful as anyone else's at the level you get it. I played my Bard until 16 and then stopped. Just not worth the time.
▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: May 26 2001 at 5:40 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I am lvl27 HIE WIZ. First I didnt know what to do with the scrolls. Someone told me. I did the quest with gate spells in approx 30 minutes. Ran to EC Tunnel and saled for 300pp in 5 minutes. Good cash isnt it?
Nipponka Angelhead
QuoteReply
# May 26 2001 at 5:40 PM Rating: Decent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I am lvl27 HIE WIZ. First I didnt know what to do with the scrolls. Someone told me. I did the quest with gate spells in approx 30 minutes. Ran to EC Tunnel and saled for 300pp in 5 minutes. Good cash isnt it?

Good luck!
▲ Page top
Great Quest
QuoteReply
# May 20 2001 at 6:54 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591758874389
10 posts
I have done this quest about 15 times, and everytime I have sold the gloves for 300pp. I am a lvl 34 wizzy now and started doing this quest when I was in my early 20's. Getting the scrolls off the gnolls is the only hard part. You have to have all 4, they are lore, so you know you got em all when you have 4 on you. Or you can right click them, and the number appears in the corner.

I do this quest to make money. If I dont want to bother killing the gnolls, I just go into SK and shout "40pp for all 4 scrolls", normally people will sell them. It takes me less than an hour to do the quest, when I have all 4 scrolls. Because I can gate to all the various places. This quest is designed for druids and wizzies, because we can travel fast. It is alot of travel, so if you are not one of those, be prepared to do some leg work.

Sometimes the gloves wont sell at all and sometimes they go like hotcakes; I have sold 3 pair in one night before (one on both my characters, and had to do quest really fast for last pair); and there have been times when I had to keep em for weeks. My wizzy still wears them, they are decent gloves - good for casters and monks. They are lore, so you can only have one pair at a time on you.

Nice as a money making product, but they are not as good as the chanters get with their SoM (talk about making cash). But not too bad, you have to be patient with the market though. Just keep your eyes and ears open. I have seen people shouting - wtb Gem Inlaid Gaunts. I always sell the pair im wearing, because I know I can get quest done again for under 50pp and an hour's worth of time.

Philibus Sophisticus
34 Wizard of Bertox
▲ Page top
RE: Great Quest
QuoteReply
# Sep 22 2001 at 9:17 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591768617253
8 posts
I agree. I did the quest for the first time today, and it took just a little over an hour to complete (with a FP bank trip to buy something in EC included). It's a very easy quest for a druid or wizard, and definitely worth the time. :)

Salya Machera
26 druid, 7th hammer

▲ Page top
RE: good/evil?
QuoteReply
# Nov 06 2001 at 3:12 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Although I believe that Umvera is on some Erudite faction, unless you've killed certain Erudites, you probably won't be KOS to him. He conned indifferent to me as a Cazic-Thule worshipping iksar monk, and that's about the worst base faction you can have with most people, short of being an iksar necro or SK. So if I'm not KOS, you're probably not. Factions you do have to worry about though:
Guards of Qeynos- Obviously, if the guards kill you before you can get in the city, you can't do the quest. Killing Blackburrow gnolls or Karana bandits raises this.
Circle of Unseen Hands (Qeynos rogues): Crow's Bar is the entrance to the rogue guild, and there are several rogues in here. Killing Karana bandits will raise this.
Corrupt Qeynos Guards- some of the Qeynos guards are on this faction, and three guards who are in front of Crow's Bar in the daytime are definitely on this faction. Including Lieutenant Dagarok, who's a level 50 shadowknight, and can harm touch for 700 damage. So if you're KOS to the corrupt guards, either come here at night or get invised in and out of the bar. Doing the note quests (Harkin to Janam and Nestral to Rebby) for the FP rogue guild will raise this.
Storm Guard (Kaladim warrior guild, and Kaladim/Butcherblock guards) Butcherblock is full of dwarf guards, and Durkis Battlemore regards people the same way Storm Guard does, despite Durkis's independence of faction. Kill Runnyeye goblins or Crushbone orcs to raise the faction if necessary.
Freeport Militia: West and East Freeport guards are guarded by the militia. Doing the note quests I've mentioned for the Corrupt Qeynos Guards raises this. If you're KOS to the militia to begin with, create a mule (human rogue either agnostic or Bristlebane worshipping) to do the note quest (Nestral to Rebby is more practical, as Rebby's response is not only not lore, but stackable). Gather the responses (Rebby's rat whiskers) and transfer them to your charaxter to turn in to Nestral.

Jheron Falkis also regarded me indifferently. Except for Durkis, evil races probably have enough faction already with the quest NPCs to do this quest, all you have to worry about is the factions of people near these NPCs who will attack you if you get too close to them.
▲ Page top
RE: good/evil?
QuoteReply
# Jun 19 2001 at 12:57 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I've done most of this quest and I'm a Dark Elf. You can get the quest and do it as long as the NPCs con at least dubious to you (i.e. you're not KoS). However, I'm KoS on Faydwer, so I'm trying to raise my faction to finish the quest.

Here's the question: Can an evil race raise their faction high enough to con dubious to dwarfs? Can this be done without trashing your faction in your home town?

Any info would be appreciated.
▲ Page top
RE: good/evil?
QuoteReply
# Jul 08 2001 at 9:21 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247487516
28 posts
Yes, its very simple, just kill lots and lots of Orcs in CB, or lots of golins in BB, or lots and lots of Aviaks, and that will raise a lot of faydwer factions, but not any Neriak as far as i know
Khallim Bloodhelm
a Dwarf,(duh)
▲ Page top
RE: good/evil?
QuoteReply
# Oct 24 2001 at 2:32 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
The most efficient way to raise Kaladim, without hurting any DE factions, is to go to Runnyeye and kill every goblin you see for a few hours. Eventually you won't be KOS to the guards and warriors. Unlike Crushbone orcs, you can raise dwarf faction killing RE goblins without hurting Indigo Brotherhood (the Neriak warrior guild).

Edited, Thu Sep 12 20:39:54 2002
▲ Page top
RE: good/evil?
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2001 at 7:29 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591761195679
203 posts
Don't do it by killing orcs in CB. Orcs in CB give you a hit against the Indigo Brotherhood, the Neriak warrior guild. Remember Ambassador D`Vinn, the Dark Elf?
▲ Page top
RE: good/evil?
QuoteReply
# Sep 06 2002 at 1:56 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Yes, but unless you're a dark elf warrior, that shouldn't really matter, as the warrior guild is easy to avoid in Neriak. The guards in Neriak and Nektulos are NOT on Indigo Brotherhood (they're on Dread Guard Outer/Inner). Ambassador D'Vinn is about the only one on this faction you'll ever meet outside of the Neriak warrior guild. Dark elves (other than warrirors) need not concern themselves with this faction (as long as you don't do something stupid, and march right into the warrior guild in Neriak!)

Edited, Fri Sep 6 02:42:50 2002

__DEL__1592247364644
10 posts
could a monk do this quest at lvl 23?

# May 05 2001 at 8:48 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750976102
281 posts
yeah im sure you can at lvl 23 just join a gnoll group in SK collect the 4 proper scrolls thats the hard part . the scrolls are all lore and many times through out the day you will see ppl shouting scroll on gnoll at N spire etc...

▲ Page top
#Anonymous, Posted: Apr 28 2001 at 7:44 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Kinda off the topic, but I just hit lvl 23 last night and I can solo gnolls <just barely>. Druid on torvi - Loving the gnolls for the exp.
Price
QuoteReply
# Apr 27 2001 at 1:17 PM Rating: Excellent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
The price on these seems to vary widely. I've seen them sell for anywhere from 100pp to 300pp, and there are always plenty to be had, so they're not rare. A mid-20s level druid could solo the Tesch Mals without too much trouble, and because they are lore, people will often sell or give away the scrolls.
▲ Page top
RE: Price
QuoteReply
# Nov 26 2001 at 4:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
Minor correction, but an important one: the scrolls drop off of Tesch MAS gnolls, not Tesch MAL gnolls. I wasted quite a bit of time on the wrong kind of gnolls before I realized my mistake. Almost all of the gnolls in SK just outside of Paw are Tesch Mas, and they're quite common in the first room (between the SK zone and the first set of doors) inside Splitpaw.
